claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is determined separately for each child, so let's start with some basic information.

**First question: Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. Her name is Alex, and she's 16 years old. She lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 16 years old, I need to ask a few more questions to determine eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** This would include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- T-levels
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to level 3
- Home education (if started before they turned 16)
- Certain training courses

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she's studying for her A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is studying A-levels, I need to ask a couple more questions.

**Does Alex work 24 hours a week or more?** (This would include paid employment or self-employment)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she doesn't work at all. She's focused on her studies.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to ask about your own circumstances:

**Are you responsible for Alex?** This means you're responsible for her care and you live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for her. She lives with me and I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Do you normally live in the UK?** (Not just visiting temporarily)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live here normally - this is my home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more important question:

**Is there anyone else who also has Alex living with them, or is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for her?** (For example, if you share care with another parent or if someone else has already claimed)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no one else. Alex lives with me full-time, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me make sure I have everything correct:

- You have one child, **Alex**, who is **16 years old**
- Alex is studying **A-levels at sixth form college**
- Alex does **not work** (so not working 24 hours a week or more)
- You are **responsible for Alex** and she **lives with you full-time**
- You **normally live in the UK** (in Manchester)
- **No one else is claiming** Child Benefit for Alex

Based on the eligibility criteria, I can now provide you with a determination.

**Good news - you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex qualifies as an eligible child because she is 16 years old and in approved full-time education (A-levels)
- You are responsible for her and she lives with you
- You live in the UK
- No one else is claiming for her

Alex can continue to be covered by Child Benefit until she turns 20, as long as she remains in approved education or training and doesn't work 24 hours a week or more.

**Important note about the High Income Child Benefit Charge:** If you or your partner earns £60,000 or more per year, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through the tax system via the High Income Child Benefit Charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim and choose whether to receive payments or not.

Would you like any information about how to claim, or do you have any other questions?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great news, thank you so much for explaining everything so clearly! I think I have all the information I need for now. I really appreciate your help.
